task_completion.description: Deploy the Multiparameter Monitor simulated device adapter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An OpenICE device adapter window is visible showing 'Multiparameter' running or active.

task_completion.description: Deploy the Capnography or CO2 simulated device adapter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An OpenICE device adapter window is visible showing 'CO2_Simulator' or Capnography running or active.

task_completion.description: Deploy the Pulse Oximeter simulated device adapter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An OpenICE device adapter window is visible showing 'PO_Simulator' or Pulse Oximeter running or active.

task_completion.description: Launch the Vital Signs application and verify data flow/waveforms for the devices.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenICE Supervisor 'Vital Signs' application window is open, displaying active waveforms or numerical data for the deployed devices.
A text editor is open showing the file 'sedation_monitoring_protocol.txt' saved on the Desktop, or the file icon is visible on the Desktop.

task_completion.description: The protocol document contains the five required sections.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains the exact headings: 'Monitoring Requirements', 'Device Configuration', 'Pre-Procedure Checklist', 'Alarm Thresholds', and 'Emergency Response'.

task_completion.description: The 'Pre-Procedure Checklist' section contains at least 5 items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Under the 'Pre-Procedure Checklist' heading in the text file, there is a list of at least 5 distinct items (e.g., bulleted or numbered).

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenICE graphical interface to deploy devices and view vital signs.
integrity.visual_evidence: OpenICE GUI windows are visible (device adapters, Supervisor app) rather than just terminal commands mocking the output.

integrity.description: The protocol document was actually written to the specified path.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor title bar shows the correct path '/home/ga/Desktop/sedation_monitoring_protocol.txt' or the file is clearly saved on the Desktop.

integrity.description: The agent generated meaningful content for the protocol document rather than just copying the prompt.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text under the headings contains actual medical/procedural protocol text (e.g., specific alarm limits, emergency steps) rather than just repeating the task description.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires configuring three specific simulated devices in OpenICE: 'Multiparameter' (hemodynamics/ECG), 'CO2_Simulator' (capnography/ventilation), and 'PO_Simulator' (pulse oximeter/oxygenation). The 'Vital Signs' application in OpenICE Supervisor must be used to view the data flow and waveforms. The protocol document must be saved to /home/ga/Desktop/sedation_monitoring_protocol.txt and contain exactly these five section headers: 'Monitoring Requirements', 'Device Configuration', 'Pre-Procedure Checklist', 'Alarm Thresholds', and 'Emergency Response'. The Pre-Procedure Checklist must have at least 5 items. Scoring breakdown: Device creation (32 pts total), App launch/data flow (18 pts), Protocol file creation and content (50 pts total).
